name: ImmunityBio, Inc.
business: ImmunityBio is a biotechnology company that develops and commercializes next-generation immunotherapies, primarily ANKTIVA (nogapendekin alfa inbakicept), a first-in-class IL-15 receptor superagonist antibody-cytokine fusion protein, for the treatment of BCG-unresponsive non-muscle invasive bladder cancer (NMIBC) and other oncology indications. The company also has a pipeline of product candidates including cytokine fusion proteins, DNA and vaccine vectors, and NK-cell therapies targeting various cancers and infectious diseases.
sellsTo: Cancer patients with BCG-unresponsive NMIBC CIS
Patients with metastatic NSCLC (conditional approval in Saudi Arabia)
U.S. and international healthcare providers and hospitals

exposures.sentence: ANKTIVA is our lead biologic product and a first-in-class IL-15 receptor superagonist antibody-cytokine fusion protein.

catalysts.event: FDA approval of ANKTIVA for BCG-unresponsive NMIBC CIS
catalysts.sentence: The FDA approved ANKTIVA with BCG in April 2024 for the treatment of adult patients with BCG-unresponsive NMIBC CIS with or without papillary tumors.
In July 2025, ANKTIVA received approval from the MHRA for the UK

catalysts.event: SFDA approval in Saudi Arabia for NMIBC
catalysts.sentence: in January 2026 received accelerated approval from the SFDA in Saudi Arabia in the same indication.

catalysts.event: EMA conditional marketing authorization in EU
catalysts.sentence: In February 2026, the European Commission granted conditional marketing authorization in the EU for ANKTIVA in combination with BCG for adult patients with BCG-unresponsive NMIBC CIS with or without papillary tumors.

In January 2026, the SFDA granted conditional approval of ANKTIVA for use in combination with immune CPIs for the treatment of adult patients with metastatic NSCLC whose disease has progressed following standard-of-care therapy.

catalysts.event: Pivotal data readout for BCG-naive NMIBC Cohort A
catalysts.sentence: Pivotal data readout expected in the second half of 2026.

catalysts.event: BLA submission target for BCG-naive NMIBC
catalysts.sentence: BLA submission target set for late 2026 to early 2027.

catalysts.event: Full enrollment expected for Phase 3 ResQ201A trial
catalysts.sentence: Full enrollment expected in the first half of 2027.

tamClaims.claim: Bladder cancer is the sixth most diagnosed cancer in the U.S., with an estimated 84,530 new cases and 17,870 deaths expected in 2026.
tamClaims.sentence: According to the American Cancer Society, bladder cancer is the sixth most diagnosed cancer in the U.S., with an estimated 84,530 new cases and 17,870 deaths expected in 2026.

tamClaims.claim: Lung cancer is the second most common cancer in the U.S., with an estimated 229,410 new cases and 124,990 deaths in 2026.
tamClaims.sentence: According to the American Cancer Society, lung cancer is the second most common cancer in the U.S., with an estimated 229,410 new cases and 124,990 deaths in 2026.

tamClaims.claim: An estimated 2,114,850 new cancer cases will be diagnosed in the U.S. in 2026, approximately 90% of which are solid tumors.
tamClaims.sentence: According to the American Cancer Society, an estimated 2,114,850 new cancer cases will be diagnosed in the U.S. in 2026.

tamClaims.claim: Lymphopenia affects a substantial portion of U.S. cancer patients annually.
tamClaims.sentence: Based on published literature and internal estimates derived from American Cancer Society incidence data and treatment patterns, lymphopenia affects a substantial portion of U.S. cancer patients annually.
